title: ByteWolf Target Observation v0.1
description: Where a detected target sits relative to the vehicle, projected from a down-facing camera detection using the vehicle's own position and altitude. It describes a target; it never commands. A consumer acts only on a valid, fresh observation.

captured_at.description: RFC 3339, UTC, with an explicit offset. When the source frame was captured; age is measured from here.

validity.description: 'valid': the target was seen and projected on a trusted, near-nadir frame. 'invalid': it was seen but cannot be trusted -- untrusted detection, unknown altitude, or too much tilt. 'missing': no target. 'stale' is derived from age.

frame.description: The offset is local north-east-down metres relative to the vehicle at capture, matching GOTO_LOCAL.

range_m.description: Slant range from the camera to the target on the ground.

horizontal_uncertainty_m.description: Grows with altitude: a pixel error projects to a larger ground error the higher the vehicle flies.

global_position.description: Present only when the vehicle's own GPS was supplied, so the target has an absolute fix as well.

then.description: A valid observation must carry the target it resolved.

else.description: An invalid or missing observation carries no target, so absence cannot be read as a fix at the origin.
